intro: Olsynium douglasii, also known as Douglas' mariposa lily, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Olsynium douglasii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory infections and promote overall well-being.
